LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND HEATHER FONG'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. DISCUSSED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED (THIRD AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND) IN PART AND OVERRULED (SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION) IN PART. AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF HAS ALLEGED MINISTERIAL ACTS IN PARAGRAPHS 14, 15, 16, 17 AND 39. THESE ACTS ARE NOT SUBJECT TO THE IMMUNITIES IN GOVERNMENT CODE SECTIONS 820.2 (DISCRETIONARY ACTS), 821.6 (INVESTIGATORY PROCESS) AND CIVIL CODE SECTION 47(A) OFFICIAL ACTS PRIVILEGE. TAYLOR V CITY OF LOS ANGELES DEPT. OF WATER AND POWER (2006) 144 CAL APP 4TH 1216, 1239. THE ACTS ALLEGED IN THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION ARE PROTECTED BY CIVIL CODE SECTION 47(A). LEAVE TO AMEND IS NOT GRANTED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR ORDER CONTINUING TRIAL DATE AND DATE FOR FILING DISPOSTIVE MOTIONS; MEMO OF P&A, POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, DECLARATION FILED BY DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO FONG, HEATHER AN INDIVIDUAL

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND HEATHER FONG'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: SUSTAINED AS TO SECOND AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION WHICH WERE ADDED CONTRARY TO THE ORDER OF THE COURT; ABSENT THE FILING AND GRANTING OF A REQUEST FOR LEAVE TO AMEND, PLAINTIFF MAY NOT AMEND THESE CAUSES OF ACTION. SUSTAINED WITH FIVE DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE THIRD, FIFTH AND SIXTH CAUSES OF ACTION. AS PLEAD (IN PARTICULAR PARAGRAPHS SIX AND NINE THROUGH TWELVE) ALL OF CHIEF FONG'S ACTIONS WERE IN CONNECTION WITH THE DISCIPLINARY PROCESS AND ARE SUBJECT TO A QUALIFIED IMMUNITY. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO PLEAD A COHERENT SET OF FACTS THAT ALLEGE A CLAIM NOT SUBJECT TO IMMUNITY. COURT SETS THE BRIEFING SCHEDULE ON THE NEXT DEMURRER: DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL TO FILE THE DEMURRER BY MARCH 14, 2008, OPPOSITION DUE ON MARCH 18, 2008, REPLY DUE ON MARCH 31, 2008. COURT RESERVES MARCH 25, 2008 FOR THE HEARING. DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL TO FILE A SEPARATE NOTICE OF MOTION SETTING THE HEARING DATE ON MARCH 25, 2008. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-25-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND HEATHER FONG'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT.
APPEARANCES: LARRY HECIMOVICH, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY (OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY, 415.554.3933) APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT. THERE ARE NO OTHER APPEARANCES. COUNSEL INDICATED THAT HE DID GIVE NOTICE TO OPPOSING COUNSEL.
RULING: DISCUSSED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED (THIRD AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND) IN PART AND OVERRULED (SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION) IN PART. AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF HAS ALLEGED MINISTERIAL ACTS IN PARAGRAPHS 14, 15, 16, 17 AND 39. THESE ACTS ARE NOT SUBJECT TO THE IMMUNITIES IN GOVERNMENT CODE SECTIONS 820.2 (DISCRETIONARY ACTS), 821.6 (INVESTIGATORY PROCESS) AND CIVIL CODE SECTION 47(A) OFFICIAL ACTS PRIVILEGE. TAYLOR V CITY OF LOS ANGELES DEPT. OF WATER AND POWER (2006) 144 CAL APP 4TH 1216, 1239. THE ACTS ALLEGED IN THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION ARE PROTECTED BY CIVIL CODE SECTION 47(A). LEAVE TO AMEND IS NOT GRANTED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (302/PJM/VC) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-05-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND HEATHER FONG'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.
APPEARANCES: DOW PATTEN, ESQUIRE (LAW OFFICES OF WAUKEEN Q. MCCOY, 415.675.7705) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. LARRY HECIMOVICH, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY (OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY, 415. 554.3933) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: SUSTAINED AS TO SECOND AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION WHICH WERE ADDED CONTRARY TO THE ORDER OF THE COURT; ABSENT THE FILING AND GRANTING OF A REQUEST FOR LEAVE TO AMEND, PLAINTIFF MAY NOT AMEND THESE CAUSES OF ACTION. SUSTAINED WITH FIVE DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE THIRD, FIFTH AND SIXTH CAUSES OF ACTION. AS PLEAD (IN PARTICULAR PARAGRAPHS SIX AND NINE THROUGH TWELVE) ALL OF CHIEF FONG'S ACTIONS WERE IN CONNECTION WITH THE DISCIPLINARY PROCESS AND ARE SUBJECT TO A QUALIFIED IMMUNITY. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO PLEAD A COHERENT SET OF FACTS THAT ALLEGE A CLAIM NOT SUBJECT TO IMMUNITY. COURT SETS THE BRIEFING SCHEDULE ON THE NEXT DEMURRER: DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL TO FILE THE DEMURRER BY MARCH 14, 2008, OPPOSITION DUE ON MARCH 18, 2008, REPLY DUE ON MARCH 31., 2008. COURT RESERVES MARCH 25, 2008 FOR THE HEARING. DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL TO FILE A SEPARATE NOTICE OF MOTION SETTING THE HEARING DATE ON MARCH 25, 2008. (302/PJM/VC) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
